title: Annie Gagnon-Larocque
content:
## Annie Gagnon-Larocque
Partner | Real Property and Planning Group Practice Lead (Québec Region)
People.ContactEmail aglarocque@mccarthy.ca
Annie Gagnon-Larocque is a thorough, sophisticated and visionary lawyer with market-leading experience in commercial real estate transactions.
A partner in our Real Property and Planning Group in Montréal, Annie is recognized throughout the industry for her business acumen and expertise in complex transactions, and serves as the Practice Lead of our Real Property and Planning Group for the Québec region.
Annie represents and advises sophisticated commercial property developers on a variety of transactions notably real estate acquisitions, real estate financings, property development, commercial leasing, and joint venture transactions. She represents financial institutions and public and private companies in the senior housing, retail and hotel industries.

#### Annie served as lead lawyer on the following transactions:
- Quartier RoyalMount Limited Partnership /Carbonleo in connection with the development and construction financing of the Royalmount Project.
- Dév Méta Inc., through one of its affiliates, in its sale of Marquise / Rubis Multi-Residential Towers to BentallGreenOak for C$150 million.
- Hydro-Québec Pension Plan and Carbonleo Real Estate in a joint mandate for the purchase of 50% of Quartier Dix30 shopping centre from Oxford Properties, valued at C$150 million.
- Counsel to lenders in connection with various financing transactions including a large portfolio or retirement properties, commercial office towers in Montreal, and a shopping mall in the greater Montreal area
- Groupe Petra Ltée in the acquisition of 1000 De La Gauchetière and Tour KPMG.
Her top-tier knowledge of the real estate sector makes Annie a sought-after speaker at industry conferences. In June 2024, she was one of the influential voices invited to the Sommet immobilier de Montréal to present her views on the revitalization of downtown. A Fellow of the American College of Mortgage Attorneys (ACMA), she spoke at the group’s 2023 meeting in San Diego on issues related to third-party opinion letters.
She has written for the ACMA’s publication, The Abstract . “Navigating the Complexities of Mixed-Use and Multi-Phased Projects in Canada: A Balancing Act!” appeared in the Spring 2022 edition. With John Stefaniuk, she co-authored “Canada’s Truth and Reconciliation Commission and its Calls to Action for Business and the Legal Profession” for the Fall 2024 edition.
Annie is ranked Band 1: Real Estate in Chambers Canada Guide 2025 . In addition to the ACMA, she is a member of several leading industry organizations, including the Urban Development Institute (UDI), and a member of the Real Estate Committee of the Canadian Bar Association Quebec Division. She is a past-president of CREW Montreal (Commercial Real Estate Women).
Annie is a faculty member of Concordia University’s Real Estate Management Certification program. She received her BA degree with a specialization in French Literature and Translation from McGill University in 1999 and her integrated BCL/LLB degree from McGill in 2002.
She articled at McCarthy Tétrault and has been with the firm since she was called to the Quebec Bar in 2003.
